title: Garbage collection spike
symptoms: a GC.Collect span dominates the hitch frame
gc collect / garbage collection runs mid-frame
periodic hitches correlated with allocation volume
mechanism: A managed-memory garbage collector runs a collection on the main thread, pausing all gameplay code (stop-the-world) until it finishes. The pause lands inside a frame and blows the frame budget.
diagnosis_hints: Check whether the spike span is the runtime's collector (GC.Collect or equivalent) and whether hitches recur as allocations accumulate. Correlate with managed allocations per frame.
typical_fixes: pool and reuse objects to cut per-frame managed allocations
avoid allocating in hot loops (boxing, closures, LINQ, temporary arrays)
schedule incremental or generational GC where the runtime supports it
sources: Unity Manual — Understanding the managed heap
